task.name: Expose refetch from useAccountsQuery
task.action: Edit `src/hooks/useAccountsQuery.ts`: 1. Destructure `refetch` from the `useQuery` return value alongside `data`, `isPending`, and `error`. 2. Add `refetch` to the hook's return object.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm no type errors. The hook's return type should now include `refetch`.

task.name: Expose refetch from useBudgetStatus
task.action: Edit `src/hooks/useBudgetStatus.ts`: 1. Destructure `refetch` from the `useQuery` return value alongside `data`, `isPending`, and `error`. 2. Add `refetch` to the hook's return object.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm no type errors.

task.name: Add tooltips, currency amounts in legend, and cursor-pointer
task.action: Edit `src/components/dashboard/AssetCategoriesChart.tsx`: 1. Import `{ Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent }` from `@/components/ui/tooltip`. 2. Import `{ formatCurrency }` from `@/lib/format`. 3. Wrap each bar segment in a `Tooltip` + `TooltipTrigger` + `TooltipContent` set. Tooltip content: `{category.name}: ₱{formatCurrency(category.value)} ({category.percentage}%)`. 4. Add `cursor-pointer` class to each bar segment div. 5. Update the legend labels to show the currency amount: below the category name, show `₱{formatCurrency(category.value)}` on one line and `{category.percentage}%` on another.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify tooltips appear on bar segment hover and legend shows ₱ amounts.

task.name: Add animated bar entrance with reduced-motion support
task.action: Edit `src/components/dashboard/AssetCategoriesChart.tsx`: 1. Import `{ motion, useReducedMotion }` from `framer-motion`. 2. Call `useReducedMotion()` at the top of the component. 3. Replace each bar segment `div` (inside the TooltipTrigger) with `motion.div`. 4. When reduced motion is false/null: add `initial={{ width: "0%" }}`, `animate={{ width: "{percentage}%" }}`, `transition={{ duration: 0.5, ease: "easeOut", delay: index * 0.05 }}`. 5. When reduced motion is true: render with static `style={{ width: "{percentage}%" }}` and no motion props. 6. Keep the existing `transition-opacity hover:opacity-80` class and inline `backgroundColor` style.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify bar segments animate in sequentially on page load. Toggle `prefers-reduced-motion` in DevTools and confirm segments appear instantly.

task.name: Improve error state with retry and convert to named export
task.action:
Edit `src/components/dashboard/AssetCategoriesChart.tsx`: 1. Import `{ AlertCircle }` from `lucide-react`. 2. Destructure `refetch` from `useAccountsQuery()`. 3. Replace the error state div contents: add `AlertCircle` icon (`h-8 w-8 text-error-600`), keep the error messages, add a "Try again" button with `cursor-pointer text-sm font-medium text-primary hover:text-primary/80 transition-colors` that calls `refetch()`. 4. Change `export default AssetCategoriesChart` to `export { AssetCategoriesChart }`.
Then edit `src/components/dashboard/NetWorthInsights.tsx`: 5. Update import: `import AssetCategoriesChart from './AssetCategoriesChart'` → `import { AssetCategoriesChart } from './AssetCategoriesChart'`.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm all imports resolve and no type errors.

phase.name: 3: BudgetStatus — Color Migration and Shared Utility

task.name: Replace inline formatCurrency and migrate color tokens
task.action: Edit `src/components/shared/BudgetStatus.tsx`: 1. Import `{ formatCurrency }` from `@/lib/format`. 2. Remove the inline `formatCurrency` function from `BudgetStatusItem`. 3. Update the progress bar color logic to use design system tokens: - No spending: `bg-secondary-400` (unchanged) - Over budget OR spending without budget: `bg-text-error` - 80–99% spent (not over budget): `bg-amber-400` - Under 80% spent (not over budget): `bg-text-success` 4. Remove `hover:text-white` from the "See All Budgets" Button component.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify progress bars use the new desaturated colors and the amber warning state appears for budgets near their limit.

task.name: Add percentage text and budget summary line
task.action: Edit `src/components/shared/BudgetStatus.tsx`: 1. In `BudgetStatusItem`, add `{progressPercentage}%` as a small text element next to or below the "out of" line. Style: `text-xs text-muted-foreground`. 2. In the main `BudgetStatus` component, calculate `onTrackCount` (budgets where `!isOverBudget && monthlyBudget > 0`) and `totalCount` (total budgets). 3. Add a subtitle line after the header: `"{onTrackCount} of {totalCount} budgets on track"`. Style: `text-xs text-muted-foreground`. 4. Only render the summary when budgets are loaded and non-empty.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify the summary line displays correctly and percentage text appears on each budget item.

phase.name: 4: BudgetStatus — Animation, Error State, and Export

task.name: Add animated progress bars with reduced-motion support
task.action: Edit `src/components/shared/BudgetStatus.tsx`: 1. Import `{ motion, useReducedMotion }` from `framer-motion`. 2. Call `useReducedMotion()` in the parent `BudgetStatus` component. 3. Pass the result as a `prefersReducedMotion` prop to each `BudgetStatusItem`. 4. Add `prefersReducedMotion: boolean` to `BudgetStatusItemProps`. 5. In `BudgetStatusItem`, replace the progress bar fill `div` with `motion.div`: - When reduced motion is false/null: `initial={{ width: "0%" }}`, `animate={{ width: "{progressWidth}%" }}`, `transition={{ duration: 0.5, ease: "easeOut" }}`. - When reduced motion is true: render with static `style={{ width: "{progressWidth}%" }}` and no motion props. 6. Keep existing classes (`h-2 rounded-full`) and the dynamic `progressColor` class.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify progress bars animate in on page load. Toggle `prefers-reduced-motion` in DevTools and confirm they appear instantly.

task.name: Improve error state with retry and convert to named export
task.action:
Edit `src/components/shared/BudgetStatus.tsx`: 1. Import `{ AlertCircle }` from `lucide-react`. 2. Destructure `refetch` from `useBudgetStatus()`. 3. Update the `ErrorState` component to accept an `onRetry` prop. 4. Add `AlertCircle` icon (`h-8 w-8 text-error-600`) to ErrorState, keep error messages, add "Try again" button with `cursor-pointer text-sm font-medium text-primary hover:text-primary/80 transition-colors` that calls `onRetry`. 5. Pass `refetch` as the `onRetry` prop when rendering `ErrorState`. 6. Change `export default BudgetStatus` to `export { BudgetStatus }`.
Then edit `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`: 7. Update import: `import BudgetStatus from '@/components/shared/BudgetStatus'` → `import { BudgetStatus } from '@/components/shared/BudgetStatus'`.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm all imports resolve and no type errors.

task.name: Lint, Build, and Final Visual Check
task.action: Run the following commands sequentially: 1. `npm run lint` — must pass with zero errors. 2. `npm run build` — must compile with zero errors. 3. Visually verify: - AssetCategoriesChart: tooltips on bar hover, animated entrance, ₱ amounts in legend, error retry works. - BudgetStatus: desaturated colors, amber warning at 80–99%, animated progress bars, summary line, percentage text, error retry works. - Both: `prefers-reduced-motion` disables animations. - No regressions in dashboard layout.
task.verify: All commands exit with code 0. Dashboard renders correctly with all improvements.
